critic_raw:
Section 6 contains an explicit test set contamination statement: 'After hyperparameter tuning on the validation set, a final check is performed using the test set to ensure robustness of model selection and to fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed.' This is not a passive check — it explicitly permits architectural modifications when 'discrepancies' are observed between validation and test performance. Any model change made after observing test set performance turns the test set into a second validation set. The reported test metric is no longer an unbiased estimate of generalization.

Preprocessing transformers (scalers, categorical encoders, vocabulary builders for visit sequences) are explicitly fit on the complete dataset before splitting. For an LSTM operating on visit sequences encoded with these vocabulary items, the vocabulary built from the full dataset includes diagnosis codes and procedure codes from test-period visits. This means the embedding matrix includes tokens that appear only in test visits, and the model can learn representations for codes it has never 'seen' at training time by leveraging the full-data vocabulary distribution.

The comparison is structurally unfair: the RNN receives sequences of 12 visits with diagnosis codes, procedure codes, medication classes, and lab summaries per visit, while the logistic regression baseline uses 'the same demographic and clinical summary features as the RNN (excluding raw sequences)' — explicitly excluding the sequential visit information. Any RNN advantage could reflect the information value of sequential visit history, not the modeling architecture. A fair test would require logistic regression to also receive aggregated features derived from the same 12-visit history.

Stratified random sampling is applied to 5 years of EHR data, violating temporal causality for a longitudinal task. The claim that this 'handles patient-level stratification to prevent patient leakage' is also ambiguous — the split is described at the observation level (patient visits), not the patient level, and a patient with 5 years of visits will have records spread across all three splits.
all_issues_raised: Section 6 explicitly describes post-test-peek architectural modification: 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' after inspecting test set results, directly contaminating the held-out evaluation.
Preprocessing vocabularies and encoders are fit on the complete dataset before splitting, providing the model with token representations for codes appearing only in test-period visits.

The RNN baseline receives sequential 12-visit histories while the logistic regression baseline explicitly does not — the comparison confounds information richness with model architecture and cannot isolate the contribution of the LSTM.

Stratified random sampling on 5 years of EHR data violates temporal causality, and the patient-level integrity claim is inconsistent with a visit-level split where the same patient's records span all three sets.
all_issues_adjudicated: Section 6 explicitly describes post-test-peek architectural modification: 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' after inspecting test set results, directly contaminating the held-out evaluation.
Preprocessing vocabularies and encoders are fit on the complete dataset before splitting, providing the model with token representations for codes appearing only in test-period visits.

The RNN baseline receives sequential 12-visit histories while the logistic regression baseline explicitly does not — the comparison confounds information richness with model architecture and cannot isolate the contribution of the LSTM.

Stratified random sampling on 5 years of EHR data violates temporal causality, and the patient-level integrity claim is inconsistent with a visit-level split where the same patient's records span all three sets.
